question_text: Red light has a wavelength of 700 nm and blue light has a wavelength of 450 nm. How many times smaller is the wavelength of blue light compared to red light? (Give your answer to 2 decimal places)
explanation: Divide the blue wavelength by the red wavelength: 450 ÷ 700 = 0.643. This shows blue light has about 64% of the wavelength of red light, or is about 36% smaller.
